Try-Works · reading comparison · same passage, body size, text optical master

Read each column to the end and notice where your eye tires first. The numbers are measured: stroke contrast (thick:thin of the lowercase o) and x-height as a fraction of the em. Lower contrast and larger x-height favour long-form reading. Fraunces stays the system face for display, headlines, and the lede; this is only about the long-form body role.

Fraunces

contrast 2.60 · x-height/em 0.482

There is a wisdom that is woe; but there is a woe that is madness. And there is a Catskill eagle in some souls that can alike dive down into the blackest gorges, and soar out of them again and become invisible in the sunny spaces. And even if he forever flies within the gorge, that gorge is in the mountains; so that even in his lowest swoop the mountain eagle is still higher than other birds upon the plain, even though they soar.

Literata

contrast 1.67 · x-height/em 0.507

There is a wisdom that is woe; but there is a woe that is madness. And there is a Catskill eagle in some souls that can alike dive down into the blackest gorges, and soar out of them again and become invisible in the sunny spaces. And even if he forever flies within the gorge, that gorge is in the mountains; so that even in his lowest swoop the mountain eagle is still higher than other birds upon the plain, even though they soar.

Source Serif 4

There is a wisdom that is woe; but there is a woe that is madness. And there is a Catskill eagle in some souls that can alike dive down into the blackest gorges, and soar out of them again and become invisible in the sunny spaces. And even if he forever flies within the gorge, that gorge is in the mountains; so that even in his lowest swoop the mountain eagle is still higher than other birds upon the plain, even though they soar.
